headline: 3 Reasons Exxon, Other Oil Stocks Are Rising as Iran War Drags On
summary: Exxon  Mobil and other 0il-and-gas company stocks gained early Monday amid escalating tensions in the Middle East and as investors positioned themselves for the possibility of extended supply disruptions.  Oil exploration and production companies  Exxon Chevron  and  Occidental Petroleum  were all up in premarket trading—by 0.7%, 0.7%, and 1.2%, respectively.  Natural gas companies had a similarly strong start to the week.

headline: Chevron’s Hess Deal And New Projects Test Valuation As Oil Prices Surge
summary: Chevron (NYSE:CVX) has closed its acquisition of Hess, adding a stake in the Stabroek block in Guyana. The company has secured exclusive negotiation rights for Iraq’s West Qurna 2 oilfield. Chevron has resumed Venezuelan oil exports, sending its first shipment since late 2024. These moves come as oil prices react to fresh conflicts and supply disruptions in the Middle East. Chevron enters this period with its shares at $189.94 and a return of 21.8% year to date as of the latest close. Over...
